mnemonics: Hybrid apps are like ICE CREAM: Ice Cream (Code) Runs Everywhere, Almost Magically.
keyPoints: Native apps are built for a specific platform (iOS or Android) using platform-specific languages.
Hybrid apps are built with web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and run inside a native container on any device.
The main difference is that native apps have direct access to all device hardware and APIs, while hybrid apps rely on a bridge.
commonMistakes: Assuming a hybrid app is just a website; it is packaged as a native app and can be distributed through app stores.
Thinking 'cross-platform' and 'hybrid' are the same; native cross-platform tools (like Flutter) compile to native code, which is different.
Underestimating the performance cost of the 'bridge' in a hybrid architecture compared to a purely native app.
